title: Dominion Energy Announces Successful Remarketing Of Series A Cumulative Perpetual Convertible Preferred Stock
text: RICHMOND, Va. , May 31, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Dominion Energy, Inc. (NYSE: D) announced today that it has successfully remarketed its Series A Cumulative Perpetual Convertible Preferred Stock (the Series A Preferred Stock).

title: UGI Benefits From Systematic Investments and Debt Management
text: UGI Corporation (UGI), with systematic investments, proper debt management and accretive acquisitions, is poised for growth. However, unfavorable weather and higher interest rates are headwinds.

title: How Are Utility ETFs Reacting to Decent Q1 Earnings Results?

title: 10 Retirement Stocks to Buy After Age 50

title: Dominion Energy's (D) Q1 Earnings & Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Dominion Energy's (D) first-quarter earnings were lower than expected. Dominion maintained its long-term capital expenditure plan, which was focused toward lowering emission.

title: Dominion Energy (D) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Miss Estimates
text: Dominion Energy (D) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -0.84% and 0.16%, respectively, for the quarter ended March 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Dominion Energy Announces First-Quarter 2022 Earnings
text: First-quarter 2022 GAAP net income of $0.83 per share; operating earnings of $1.18 per share Company initiates second quarter 2022 operating earnings guidance of $0.70 to $0.80 per share Company affirms full-year 2022 operating earnings guidance as well as other long-term earnings and dividend guidance RICHMOND, Va. , May 5, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Dominion Energy (NYSE: D) today announced an unaudited net income determined in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (reported earnings) for the three months ended March 31, 2022, of $711 million ($0.83 per share) compared with net income of $1.0 billion ($1.23 per share) for the same period in 2021.

title: Utility Stocks' Q1 Earnings Due on May 5: D, EVRG, PPL & More
text: Let's focus on utility stocks D, EVRG, PPL, ED and SRE that are scheduled to release first-quarter earnings on May 5.
